input.background: A jury having heard the evidence in a criminal case, is debating whether to render a verdict of guilty or not guilty. Because it is worse to convict an innocent person of a crime they did not commit than let a guilty person go free, each case starts with a presumption of innocence. This is analogous to the presumption that $H_0$ is true in a statistical hypothesis test. Other analogies exist.
input.question: Which type of decision is analogous to a type I error?

output.answer: Declaring an innocent person guilty (b) is analogous to a type I error.

presumption_of_innocence.value: Presumption that the defendant is not guilty
presumption_of_innocence.description: The initial assumption that the defendant is innocent, analogous to the null hypothesis being true in hypothesis testing.

type_I_error.description: A type I error occurs when the null hypothesis is true but is rejected, analogous to convicting an innocent person in a jury trial.
